content: If temperature of Sun $$=6000 \mathrm{~K}$$, radius of Sun is $$7.2 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~km}$$, radius of Earth $$=6000 \mathrm{~km}$$ and distance between Earth and Sun $$=15 \times 10^7 \mathrm{~km}$$. Find intensity of light on Earth.
explanation:
Given, temperature of sun, $$T_s=6000 \mathrm{~K}$$

Radius of sun, $$R_s=7.2 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~km}=7.2 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~m}$$

Radius of earth, $$R_e=6000 \mathrm{~km}=600 \times 10^3 \mathrm{~m}$$

Distance between earth and sun,

$$d=15 \times 10^7 \mathrm{~km}=15 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{~m}$$

Intensity of light on the earth,

$$\begin{aligned} I & =\frac{\text { Total energy emitted by sun }}{4 \pi d^2} \times\left(\pi R_e{ }^2\right) \\ & =\frac{\sigma\left(T_s^4 \cdot 4 \pi R_s^2 \times \pi R_e{ }^2\right)}{4 \pi d^2}=\frac{\sigma T_s^4 R_s{ }^2 \times \pi R_e{ }^2}{d^2} \\ & \begin{aligned} & =\frac{5.67 \times 10^{-8} \times(6000)^4 \times\left(7.2 \times 10^8\right)^2 \times 3.14 \times (6000\times10^3)^2}{\left(15 \times 10^{10}\right)^2} \\ & =19.2 \times 10^{16} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

content:
Assertion : In both radioactivity and photoelectric effect electrons may be ejected.

Reason : In photoelectric effect and radioactivity emission occurs only of unstable elements.

options.content: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true, but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true, but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.

explanation:
In radioactivity, electrons are emitted in the form of $$\beta\left({ }_{-1} e^{\circ}\right)$$ particles. It is also called negative $$\beta$$-decay, where an unstable nucleus emits an energetic electron and an anti-neutrino and a neutron in the nucleus becomes a proton that remains in the product nucleus.

In photoelectric effect, when light rays of frequency greater than or equal to threshold frequency incident on metal surface, then electrons are emitted from metal surface. This phenomenon is called photoelectric effect. For photoelectric emission it is not necessary that element is unstable but for radioactivity, it is necessary that elements are unstable. Hence, assertion is true but reason is false.

content: The graph between the energy log E of an electron and its de-Broglie wavelength log $$\lambda$$ will be
explanation:
As we know that, wavelength of a particle

$$\begin{aligned} & \lambda =\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m E}}=\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{E}} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \log \lambda =\log \left(\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{E}}\right) \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \log \lambda =\log \frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m}}+\log \frac{1}{E^{1 / 2}} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \log \lambda =\log \left(\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m}}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log E \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \log \lambda =-\frac{1}{2} \log E+\log \frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m}} \end{aligned}$$

So, equation of straight line is $$y=m x+c$$.

This is equation of line with slope $$-\frac{1}{2}$$.

content:
Assertion : Photoelectric effect can take place only with an electron bound in the atom.

Reason : Electron is a fermion whereas proton is a boson.

options.content: Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
Both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
Assertion is true but reason is false
Both assertion and reason are false.

explanation: The photoelectric effect is the emission of electrons (called photoelectrons) when light strikes a metal surface. Hence, photoelectric effect can take place only with an electron bound in the atom. Electron is a lepton whereas proton is fermion.
